query: Draft an RFC for moving our search index from Elasticsearch to OpenSearch. Cover motivation, current state, proposed approach, migration plan, and rollback. I'll want to give per-section feedback once you're done.
ok i like that response let me give feedback on each part
make that targetable

Write a 2026 hiring plan for the data platform team — headcount by sub-team, levels mix (L4/L5/L6 split), sourcing channels we'll actually use, the interview loop changes I want to test, and budget asks. I want to iterate on each section.
lemme refine each of those OKRs one by one - they're not quite right yet

Put together a Q4 product roadmap covering platform stability work, the merchant onboarding flow rebuild, and the new analytics surface. Owners + rough sizing per item. I'll mark up each section before we share it with the exec team.
I want to refine each role description in that resume draft you just wrote, one at a time
the announcement you drafted - i want to iterate on each paragraph before we send it to the all-hands list
write me a brand voice doc for Acme Notebook covering tone, vocabulary do's/don'ts, example phrases, and what we never say. then let me give per-section feedback so we can tighten it.
Can you put that on-call runbook into a format where I can mark up each step? Some of them need rework.
fix the second bullet to say 'within 24 hours' instead of 'within a day' - that's the only change
Draft an RFC for moving our search index from Elasticsearch to OpenSearch. Cover motivation, current state, proposed approach, migration plan, and rollback.
Write a Python script that migrates users from AWS Cognito to Auth0 in batches of 1000, with a --dry-run flag and a checkpoint file so we can resume if it crashes mid-run.
Give me a 2-paragraph summary of what bitemporal data modeling is and when you'd actually reach for it

This test keeps flaking in CI - I want to iterate on it until it's deterministic. Can you walk through what could cause the non-determinism? ```python
def test_user_signup():
    user = create_user('test@example.com')
    assert user.id == 1
```

Build me a comparison table across these four object storage options: S3, R2, GCS, B2. Columns I care about: per-GB price, egress cost, S3 API compatibility, region availability, and presigned URL TTL limits.
I'm trying to pick between Auth0 and Clerk for a new B2B SaaS. Want to brainstorm the trade-offs with me?
in that Q3 plan you wrote earlier, the second bullet under 'on-call' - just delete it, we're not doing that one this quarter
Can you give me feedback on this paragraph? 'Acme Notebook is the best notebook for thinking through hard problems.'
